name: ASAP3
description: 3rd-gen ASAP sensor with large fluorescence modulation and fast kinetics.

spectrum.name: ASAP3
spectrum.note: No one-photon excitation/emission spectrum is published for ASAP3: the Villette 2019 paper reports only imaging filter settings (ex 490/10, em 525/40 — a collection band, not a peak) and no spectral figure, and FPbase has no ASAP entry. ASAP3 shares the cpsfGFP-OPT (superfolder GFP) chromophore of ASAP1/ASAP2s (derived from ASAP2f via L146G/S147T/S150G/H151D/R414Q; EGFP was used only as a brightness control) and is not spectrally red-shifted, so the FPbase Superfolder GFP excitation/emission curve is used as a fluorophore proxy. Replaces a fabricated curve that was the FPbase EGFP spectrum red-shifted +16 nm (ex) / +14 nm (em) to fake peaks of 505/525.

voltage.name: ASAP3
voltage.note: Derived curve: the steady-state response SHAPE was digitized from the green ASAP3 Boltzmann in Fig. 2A (normalized (F−Fmin)/(Fmax−Fmin), V½ ≈ −88 mV), then converted to ΔF/F relative to −70 mV by solving the absolute floor Fmin/Fmax = 0.23 from the Table 1 anchor (ΔF/F per 100-mV step −70→+30 mV = −51%). The depolarizing side is well constrained; the hyperpolarizing side is steeper than the directly-measured subthreshold slope (0.46%/mV, Fig. S5E) because the Fig. 2A Boltzmann is full steady-state whereas the subthreshold measurement is kinetically attenuated.

kinetics.note: Amplitude-weighted mean of the bi-exponential fit at 32-35°C in CHO cells (Table 1). Activation: τ_fast=0.94 ms (72%), τ_slow=7.24 ms → weighted on=2.70 ms. Deactivation: τ_fast=3.79 ms (76%), τ_slow=16.0 ms → weighted off=6.72 ms.

Amplitude-weighted mean of the bi-exponential fit at 37°C in HEK293A cells imaged at 1888 fps under simultaneous voltage clamp (ASAP5 paper, Table S2; ASAP3 row, 37°C). Activation (−70→+30 mV depolarization): τ_fast=1.3 ms (73%), τ_slow=45.5 ms (27%) → weighted on=13.23 ms. Deactivation (repolarization): τ_fast=3.5 ms (81%), τ_slow=3.9 ms (19%) → weighted off=3.58 ms. Corrected from a prior entry that stored the fast components only (1.3/3.5) and mislabeled the temperature as 25°C — those values are the 37°C τ-fast pair, not a room-temperature measurement. The same table also lists a 23°C ASAP3 row (act τ_fast 3.6/63%, τ_slow 18.5; deact τ_fast 9.0/29%, τ_slow 22.8 → weighted 9.11/18.80).

Amplitude-weighted mean of the bi-exponential fit at 22-23°C in HEK293 cells (Table 1; Table S1, -70→+30 mV step). Activation: τ_fast=3.7 ms (81%), τ_slow=48 ms → weighted on=12.12 ms. Deactivation: τ_fast=16.0 ms (81%), τ_slow=102 ms → weighted off=32.34 ms.

Amplitude-weighted mean of the bi-exponential fit at 32-35°C in HEK293-Kir2.1 cells (JEDI-2P paper, Table S2; ASAP3 column). Activation: τ_fast=0.98 ms (82.4%), τ_slow=20.9 ms → weighted on=4.49 ms. Deactivation: τ_fast=4.29 ms (86.9%), τ_slow=136 ms → weighted off=21.54 ms. Previously stored the fast components only (0.98/4.29).

Amplitude-weighted mean of the bi-exponential fit at 21-23°C in HEK293-Kir2.1 cells (JEDI-2P paper, Table S2; ASAP3 column). Activation: τ_fast=2.26 ms (78.4%), τ_slow=32.2 ms → weighted on=8.73 ms. Deactivation: τ_fast=11.0 ms (85.3%), τ_slow=112 ms → weighted off=25.85 ms. Previously stored the fast components only (2.26/11).

dynamicRangeData.note: ΔF/F per 100-mV step (-70→+30 mV) under one-photon widefield voltage clamp in HEK293 cells at 22-23°C (Table 1, Voltage Responsivity 1-Photon).
ΔF/F per 100-mV voltage-clamp step under one-photon widefield epifluorescence in HEK293 cells (in vitro testing).

ΔF/F for a true 100-mV step (-70 -> +30 mV) from the Fgreen/Fred R-V Boltzmann fit measured alongside rEstus2s (Rmax=1.15, ΔRmax=-0.85, Vhalf=-60.0, ks=40.2; Table S1). One-photon widefield voltage clamp in HEK293T cells, 23°C.

sensitivityData.note: ΔF/F per single AP (17.9 ± 1.2%) in cultured hippocampal neurons under one-photon widefield illumination at room temperature (Table 1, Voltage Responsivity 1-Photon; Fig. 2E/2F).
ASAP3-Kv optical spike amplitude (10.2% ΔF/F) under one-photon widefield blue-LED illumination at 100 mW/mm² in cultured neurons (Evans et al. 2023).
ΔF/F per single AP (17.0 ± 0.8%) in cultured hippocampal slices at 32°C under random-access multiphoton (two-photon) excitation, significantly larger than ASAP2s (11.7 ± 0.7%).
ΔF/F per single AP (8.3 ± 1.1%) in cerebellar molecular layer interneurons (very narrow spikes, FWHM 0.43 ms) at 34°C under two-photon RAMP microscopy, outperforming ASAP2s (5.3 ± 0.9%).

brightnessData.note: Molar brightness at −70 mV from Villette et al. 2019 Table 1 = 15 mM⁻¹cm⁻¹ = 15000 M⁻¹cm⁻¹ (absolute); EGFP reference 56000 × 0.60 = 33600 → 15000/33600 ≈ 0.446. Consistent with the independent 37.5 %EGFP direct measurement in Rühl et al. 2024. (Was 0.748, computed with the incorrect 20040 anchor and mis-sourced to Fig. S5D.)
Reciprocal of rEstus/ASAP3 = 1.96 read from Fig. 2C (mean molecular brightness at -120 mV relative to mKate2: ASAP3 4.06, rEstus 7.94).

photostabilityData.note: ASAP3-Kv in primary cultured mouse neurons, n=11 cells, 475-nm LED continuous illumination at 25 mW/mm² (Piatkevich et al. 2019 Ext. Fig. 5e source data, MOESM12). Illumination corrected from a prior '1500 mW/mm²' value, which was the SomArchon red-laser intensity (637 nm) mis-copied onto this green/cpGFP sensor; the green channel in that figure is 25 mW/mm². F/F0 = 0.529 at 300 s (matches the digitized photobleach entry from the same panel).

~40% photobleaching over a 1-h recording of ASAP3 in PV interneurons in mouse cortex under CW 445-nm exposure (~10 µW/mm²), versus ~10% for ASAP7y under the same conditions (green ASAP3 trace ends at ~64% at 60 min in Ext. Data Fig. 3a). Cross-measured in the ASAP7y paper (Hao et al. 2026); ASAP3, but not ASAP7y, shows reversible photobleaching (sawtooth recovery at periodic 445-nm bouts).

apWidthData.sample: HEK293A cells, optical response to a single AP-like command waveform, whole-cell voltage clamp
apWidthData.note: Optical response width (FWHM) to a single AP-like command waveform (2-ms FWHM, +30 mV peak), HEK293A whole-cell voltage clamp, fluorescence acquired at ~987 Hz (ORCA Flash 4.0 V2 sCMOS; the paper's Methods repeatedly specify ~987 fps/Hz for green-GEVI voltage imaging, not a round 1 kHz). Fig. 2f y-axis is 'Width of response to spike waveforms (ms)' and the caption states 'The width corresponds to the full-width at half-maximum' (so this IS a true FWHM, not a 30%-max width). ASAP3 (orange) bar ≈ 5.7 ms verified off the rendered panel; ASAP2s ≈ 6.7 ms. Measured alongside JEDI-1P (primary subject, ≈4.4 ms). Experiments done at room temperature (Fig. 2 caption: 'unless otherwise noted'; the 32–35 °C note applies only to panels m–o).

apWidthData.sample: Cultured rat hippocampal neurons, optical response to current-evoked single APs, current clamp, room temperature
apWidthData.note: Optical spike width at half-maximum (FWHM ≈ 12.7 ms, n = 5) of the ASAP3 optical single-AP waveform in cultured hippocampal neurons, read from the 'Optical spike width (ms)' bar chart in Fig. 2F of the origin paper (Villette et al. 2019); the co-plotted ASAP2s bar is ≈17.3 ms. Measured under one-photon widefield illumination at room temperature. samplingRate is omitted: the paper reports only a range (1–2 kHz, Table 1 footnote) rather than a single frame rate for this experiment, and this width (~12.7 ms) is dominated by ASAP3's own deactivation kinetics (τ_off ~16–32 ms at RT) rather than the frame interval (kinetics-dominated exception), so it is meaningful but not strictly comparable to frame-rate-specified widths. Distinct from the electrical AP FWHM (3.9 ± 0.2 ms) also reported in Fig. 2E, which is the command AP waveform, not the optical response.

Optical spike FWHM ≈ 3.4 ms (grey ASAP3 bar) read off the 'Spike FWHM (ms)' bar sub-panel of Fig. 3f in the 2Photron paper (Böhm et al. 2024 bioRxiv), where ASAP3 (grey) is cross-plotted with JEDI-2P (green, ≈1.85 ms) and 2Photron-ST552 (red, ≈1.42 ms). ASAP3 data are from Villette et al. 2019 (n=23 cells) as noted in the caption. Two-photon ULoVE recording (3.75 kHz). No numeric ASAP3 value is given in the text; value verified by rendering and reading the bar height against the y-axis (0–4 ms). Cross-measured in the 2Photron paper.

photobleach.note: Cross-GEVI photobleaching from the same Fig. S4 46 mW/mm² panel as JEDI-1P (measured alongside it; 470/24-nm widefield, HEK293T, n=11). The published trace is brightness relative to ASAP2s; the ASAP3 (orange) curve was re-normalized to its own first frame → F/F0. Half-life t50 ≈ 295 s, extrapolated well beyond the 120 s window (curve ends at F/F0≈0.70; relies on the slow component τ2≈562 s). Stored as t50 (not t75) to match the figure's other curves, whose 75% crossings fall in the shared rapid-initial-photobleach transient. NOTE: in fractional (F/F0) terms ASAP3 bleaches slightly LESS than the much brighter JEDI-1P here — JEDI-1P's advantage in this figure is its larger photon budget (absolute brightness), not lower fractional bleaching.

In-vivo 1P photostability digitized from Fig. 2b (ASAP3-Kv, green), hippocampal SST+ interneurons, continuous 453-nm widefield at 100 mW/mm², acquired in 11-s bouts time-binned to 500 fps. Noisy in-vivo trace; ends at F/F0≈0.77 at 75 s, so t75≈95 s is extrapolated just past the window. Measured alongside ASAP4b/ASAP4e (same figure → cross-overlay).

Cross-GEVI entry from the JEDI-2P origin paper (primary subject JEDI-2P): 1P photobleaching of ASAP3 in HEK293-Kir2.1 cells, widefield 1PM at 15 mW/mm² over 5 min (Fig. S4G, orange curve). Digitized by HSV color separation. Large fast-bleach initial drop (to ~0.64), so the t75% crossing is transient-dominated → half-life t50 ≈ 59 s stored instead. Biexponential fit. Companion curves in the same panel: JEDI-2P, ASAP2s.

Cross-GEVI photobleaching from the SomArchon paper (Piatkevich et al. 2019, primary subject SomArchon). ASAP3-Kv (cpGFP, KV2.1-targeted) in primary cultured mouse neurons (n=11), 475 nm LED at 25 mW/mm² continuous illumination, digitized from the exact Ext. Data Fig. 5e source data (MOESM12 spreadsheet). F/F0 falls to 0.529 at 300 s and never reaches 50% in-window, so the model-free t75 = 95 s is stored (no t50; the t75 crossing is in the sustained phase, not a rapid initial transient). Biexponential fit, R²=0.998. CAVEAT (cross-overlay): the Ext. Fig. 5e panel co-plots this green sensor with SomArchon, which was imaged at 637 nm / 1,500 mW/mm² — a ~60x higher intensity at a different wavelength. intensityMWmm2 is OMITTED (no dose-scaling) because the cross-sensor comparison is NOT apples-to-apples across that wavelength/intensity gap. NB: this page's separate photostabilityData Ext.-Fig.-5e entry currently lists '1500 mW/mm²' for ASAP3-Kv, which is the SomArchon red-laser value mis-copied; the green channel here is 25 mW/mm².

Cross-GEVI in-vivo photobleaching from the ASAP7y paper (Hao et al. 2026 bioRxiv, primary subject ASAP7y). ASAP3 (green) expressed in mouse cortical PV interneurons, co-plotted with ASAP7y (yellow) over a 1-h continuous recording under CW 445-nm exposure (~10 µW/mm²); the green ASAP3 curve was digitized by color-masking with the correct 0-min (x=349 px) → 60-min (x=1619 px) tick calibration. The net trend falls from 100% to ~64% at 60 min (~40% photobleaching vs ~10% for ASAP7y, per caption). NON-MONOTONIC SAWTOOTH: ASAP3 (but not ASAP7y) undergoes REVERSIBLE photobleaching — the curve drops then recovers at each periodic 445-nm bout, so the stored trace is the median-line envelope and reflects real reversible-recovery physiology, not a digitization artifact. No biexponential fit is stored (the sawtooth makes a monotonic-decay model inappropriate, R²<0). t75 = 886 s is the first (model-free) crossing below 0.75; because of the reversible recovery the signal transiently returns above 0.75 until ~20 min. t50 never reached in-window.
